mnemonics: UCR: User, Case, Result - Remember the three core components of a use case.
keyPoints: A use case describes a set of interactions between a user (actor) and a system to achieve a specific goal.
Use cases are text-based narratives, not diagrams, though they are often represented visually in a use case diagram.
The main components include a unique name, participating actors, preconditions, a main success scenario (flow of events), and postconditions.
commonMistakes: Confusing use cases with user stories or functional requirements; use cases are more detailed and describe a flow of events.
Focusing on the user interface design within the use case instead of the actor's actions and the system's responses.
